days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Exploring the Vibrant City

timeline.title: Arrive at Hong Kong International Airport
timeline.detail: Your flight lands at HKIA. Head through customs and immigration, then take the Airport Express to Hong Kong Station.

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Icon
timeline.detail: Drop your luggage off at this chic hotel in Tsim Sha Tsui. Enjoy amenities including a rooftop pool and exquisite dining options.

timeline.title: Lunch at Tim Ho Wan
timeline.detail: Savor the world-renowned dim sum at this Michelin-starred restaurant. Be sure to try the baked BBQ pork buns and shrimp dumplings.

timeline.title: Victoria Peak
timeline.detail: Take the Peak Tram to the summit for stunning panoramic views of Hong Kong. The skyline and harbor are particularly beautiful during sunset.

timeline.title: Dinner at Bubba Gump Shrimp Co.
timeline.detail: Enjoy seafood in a fun atmosphere at this popular waterfront restaurant, boasting great views of Victoria Harbour.
timeline.mapQuery: Bubba Gump Shrimp Co., Hong Kong

timeline.title: A Symphony of Lights Show
timeline.detail: Watch the spectacular light and sound show featuring 40 buildings along the waterfront. Best viewed from Tsim Sha Tsui promenade.

localTips: Purchase an Octopus Card at the airport for easy public transportation access.
Consider making dinner reservations, especially at popular restaurants.
The Peak Tram gets busy in the evening; try to go early to avoid long queues.

days.title: Cultural Exploration of Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Heritage and Flavor

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Start your day with a Hong Kong style breakfast of scrambled eggs, toast, and milk tea at this famous diner.

timeline.title: Wong Tai Sin Temple
timeline.detail: Visit this popular temple dedicated to Wong Tai Sin, known for granting wishes. Explore the colorful halls and learn about local customs.
timeline.mapQuery: Wong Tai Sin Temple, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Try the award-winning wonton noodles at this legendary eatery. Pay attention to the broth and handmade noodles for a true taste of Hong Kong.

timeline.title: Explore Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade
timeline.detail: Stroll along this beautiful waterfront promenade with stunning views of Hong Kong Island and take photos of the skyline.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Shopping at Mong Kok
timeline.detail: Dive into the bustling streets of Mong Kok and browse famous ladies' markets for souvenirs, clothes, and local snacks.

timeline.title: Dinner at Yung Kee Restaurant
timeline.detail: Indulge in authentic Cantonese dishes, especially the roast goose, renowned as one of the top dishes in Hong Kong.

localTips: Use Google Maps to navigate the MTR; it's easy to use for English speakers.
Look out for street food stalls for affordable local snacks throughout Mong Kok.
Keep some cash handy; not all places accept cards.

days.title: Last Day in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Leisure and Departure

timeline.title: Breakfast at a Local Cha Chaan Teng
timeline.detail: Savor a traditional Hong Kong breakfast including a pineapple bun (bo lo bao) and milk tea in a casual setting.

timeline.title: Visit Kowloon Walled City Park
timeline.detail: Explore this unique park built on the site of the former Kowloon Walled City, highlighting its history and serene gardens.
timeline.mapQuery: Kowloon Walled City Park, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Sister Wah
timeline.detail: Enjoy delicious beef brisket noodles at this modest eatery loved by locals. The flavor of the broth is exceptional.

timeline.title: Relax at Central Harbourfront
timeline.detail: Take a leisurely walk at the Central Harbourfront, relax by the waterfront, and take in the scenic beauty of the harbor.

timeline.title: Last-Minute Shopping at Harbour City
timeline.detail: Browse one of the largest shopping malls in Hong Kong for unique souvenirs, designer brands, and local delicacies.

timeline.title: Head to Airport for Departure
timeline.detail: Make your way back to Hong Kong International Airport via the Airport Express for your departure flight.

localTips: Consider purchasing food items from local markets for unique gifts.
Check the flight status before heading to the airport.
Utilize the MTR for quick travel to the airport.

hotels.name: Hotel Icon
hotels.note: Chic hotel in Tsim Sha Tsui, close to MTR.
hotels.address: 17 Science Museum Road, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

budget.note: Round trip from Penang to Hong Kong.
Estimate for dining at local restaurants.
Budget for shopping at various markets.
